title: Conventions used in the BIS Quarterly Review
summary: This source is a technical reference page listing notation conventions, currency codes, and country codes used in BIS Quarterly Review publications. It contains no macro catalysts or market structure content.
key_points: This is a data dictionary/methodology page, not an analytical document
Contains definitions of abbreviations used in BIS publications (e.g., 'lhs' for left-hand scale, 'rhs' for right-hand scale)
Lists ISO currency codes (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY) and country codes (e.g., US, DE, JP)
No policy analysis, market commentary, or economic data is present
why_it_matters: This source has no relevance to macro catalysts or market structure research. It is purely administrative documentation explaining how BIS formats its data. It should not be archived as a research note.

human_review.notes: Source should be excluded from archive. It is a technical reference page explaining data formatting conventions used in BIS publications, not a research document or market analysis.

notes: IRRELEVANT TO TARGET TOPICS. This page contains only notation abbreviations, currency codes, and country codes used in BIS Quarterly Review formatting. No macro catalysts or market structure content present. Low quality source for research archive purposes.
